guide:
**Quest Name:** Bear Were Quest  
**Level:** 30  
**Class:** ALL  
**Quest NPC:** Keiv Bearclaw  
**Location:** Keiv is in the village south of CLW, North Wilderlands  
**Tail NPC:** Dorandas in the Unkemp Village, Unkempt North (S)

---

Speak with **Keiv Bearclaw** in the village south of CLW (**North Wilderlands (S)**). He is found in the same village where Sissy Harper is for the 30 class quest, just north of Sissy's door, by a well. He will send you north to roam the area around CLW (NE) until you receive a pop-up (run past CLW like you're going towards goblins). Doing so triggers two bears — **Barbiedolls Paw** & **Flurry**, each of whom con the same level as the person who triggers them. The pop-up is on a 30-minute timer. Defeat the bears and return to Keiv.

Keiv will then send you to **Anu Village** to seek out a similar pop-up. Locate the observatory in the north, and right before it is a hill that glows blue with stones on top — roam around the area until you receive a pop-up. You will again be fighting mobs similar to the first pop-up triggered event. Defeat the bears and return to Keiv.

Keiv will now ask you to speak with **Dorandas** in **Unkempt North (S)**. Log the tail quest and begin farming the appropriate bearweres until you receive the infection pop-up. If Dorandas will not speak with you, you'll need to hunt bearweres first to raise your faction with **Wards of the Dawn** to 0 (maybe 150?).

Killing **weres that con YELLOW** to you is key. Yellow-conned weres give you the greatest chance of infection. Check your level against the zone information below and ensure you are hunting Yellow-Con weres, otherwise you will only benefit from faction. If you are already infected, and faction is all you need, it is easier to hunt in a lower-level zone.

---

### On Having a Level 60 in the Group

There was always a rumor that having a higher-level toon in your group to help with the killing would mean your chances of infection would drop almost to nil. Well, here's some good information:

> **Dustyspheres** says: The rumor that having a level 60 in your low-level group affects your chance of infection just isn't true. The last 9 infections I've gotten or helped get were all with toons in one group, invised and on Auto-follow. These toons ranged from 33–39 and were on AF with a 60 bard that did all of the killing. All they did was loot tails and they still got infected. Some in just a few kills, and most before anyone even had the required 1k faction to accept the infection.

---

### "Were" to Get Infected

**Level 30–40**  
- Fort Alliance  
- North Wilderlands  
- Unkempt Glade  
- Unkempt North  

**Level 40–50**  
- Elephant Graveyard  
- Salisearaneen  

**Level 50–60**  
- Stoneburnt Mountains *(lvl 60 - confirmed)*  
- The Warrens *(lvl 51 - confirmed)*  

> *See note below on doing bear at 60.*

---

After you have become infected, you will be asked to defeat **Mirin** in **Unkempt North** (see note below). The mob cons the same level as the person who triggers him, so bring assistance.

**Orphanmaker1** says: Mirin spawns in *Unkempt North (SW)* just a bit east of the bandit village (at night time they are bearweres).

---

### Infection Check

You can also check for infection in **Oasis**. Go to the campfire near the water — several NPCs stand around it.  
- **Advisor Gerahl** checks for *general infection* (any were).  
- **Advisor Wanda** checks specifically for *bear infection*.

---

### Note on Dorandas

If you get the popup that Dorandas has something urgent to tell you:

- If all he'll do is give you the tail quest again, check first — is your faction with **Wards of the Dawn** 1000 or more? If not, get more faction by killing weres.
- If your faction is good, delete the tail quest in your quest log and re-speak to Dorandas, who will now give you the final part of your infection quest.
- When Dorandas gives his speech **do NOT take the antidote**, or you will lose your infection. **Do NOT continue to hunt tails.**

---

### Bearwere Forms

| Stat | **Bearwere** | **Feral Bearwere** | **Dire Bearwere** |
|------|---------------|--------------------|-------------------|
| **Str** | 45 | 80 | 120 |
| **Sta** | 45 | 80 | 120 |
| **HPs** | 0 | 300 | 350 |
| **Int** | -16 | -25 | -50 |
| **Notes** | — | — | Int resistable with Max AR |

The first form is a **quest reward** (lvl 30).  
The second form costs **50 cms (lvl 40)**.  
The third form costs **25 cms (lvl 50)**.

---

### Level 40 Spell Quest

> **Warning:** Many level 40 spell quest mobs are triggered, not timed spawns. If someone defeats them before you arrive, you may need to relog your quest with the NPC.

- Talk to your **class-specific NPC** in *Unkempt North (S)*. They will ask you to defeat a **Golden Gnoll** in *Crethley Manor* (SE of the undead house). It cons white, casts, and heals.  
- After defeating it, the NPC sends you to **Kierra** in *Mariel Village (C)* north of Wynd. She is a faerie at the bottom of a tree ramp.  
- Then, defeat a **Green Racer Snake** in *Unkempt Glade*. From Kierra, travel south to a lake surrounded by druids, then east to a hill where the snake spawns.  
- Alternatively, find the lake in *Unkempt South (C)*, run east to the road, follow it south to a quarry with 5 caves, then head north — the snake spawns on a hill by the road.  
- The NPC will send you to **Briggs** in *Salt Mines (C)*, located between the Slioth dock camp and the cave entrance.  
- Return to *Unkempt Glade* for your reward.

---

### Spells

**Cave Bear Strength** — *Rge, Brd, Ran, Mnk*  
> 98 STR buff that stacks with first bear form and shaman buffs.

**Growl of Battle** — *War, Sk, Pal*  
> 67 STR/STA group buff.

**Bear Nobility** — *Cl, Sha, Drd*  
> 122 CHA self-buff.

**Summon Blessed Fish** — *Mag, Nec, Wiz, Enc, Alc*  
> Summons a level 33 PoT/HoT drink item.

---

### Note: Doing Bearwere at 60

**Qnsprincess** says:

> Mirin conned deep red (with a light blue pet) to a level 60 — he's a caster mob found in *Unkempt North (S)* just west of Dorindas' hut (if you can't see the hut, you went too far). He also respawns instantly after triggering, so multiple players can do it in sequence. A level 60 alch and necro easily defeated him twice.

> My friend and I got infection in two days at level 60. We farmed **Murnf** for faction (1k with Wards of the Dawn) by killing weres near Grimm Brellhand (the 47 quest NPC). Many bearweres, wolfweres, and ratweres spawn there. Afterward, we farmed **SBM** for two hours post-server reset. After about 20 kills (white, dark blue, and light blue), we both got infected. Infection at 55+ seems much easier due to stronger mobs spawning more often.
